title: P0 fix：後端日期窗口對齊實際預報 horizon，渲染出的日卡必可點；4xx 不得重置頁面
environment: Executor: ubuntu-dev mainline worktree at /home/esgcenter0/python/Academic/trip-weather-planner using ~/venvs/python-env

context.background: User bug: clicking the 7/12 day card refreshed the whole page and reset the selection to 7/5. Management diagnosis (live-verified 2026-07-05 evening): after CWA's evening issuance the weekly dataset rolled to an 8-day horizon (7/5..7/12). The frontend strip faithfully renders every returned day, but the backend validation window is hardcoded today..today+6, so the last rendered day returns HTTP 400 — the backend rejects a date that exists in its own forecast data. The frontend error path then falls back to the default view (re-query today), which reads as a full refresh + jump to 7/5. Day-card buttons already have type=button; this is not a form-submit bug.
context.design_principle: Every day card rendered from the response MUST be clickable without error. The validation window must be derived from / aligned with the actual forecast horizon, not a hardcoded 6-day offset. And a click error must never reset the current view.

in_scope: H1 backend: widen/align the date-window validation so any date present in the currently available forecast horizon is accepted (observed horizon can be 7 or 8 days and rolls with CWA issuance). Acceptable implementations: validate against today..today+8 (Asia/Taipei) as a loose bound while pick_target_day handles absent dates gracefully, OR validate after fetch against the actual days returned — choose the cleaner one and document the choice in the Task Report. Reject clearly out-of-range dates (past dates, >10 days ahead) with 400 as before
H2 frontend: the 本週預報（共 7 天）heading count becomes dynamic — 共 N 天 based on the actual days array length

H3 frontend resilience: if a day-click re-query fails (4xx/5xx), keep the current week view and selection intact and show an inline non-blocking error message; never fall back to the default today view on a click error (full-query failures keep existing behavior)
H4 tests: backend accepts a today+7 date when the horizon contains it; strip renders N cards for N days; day-click failure keeps view intact (no reset), inline error shown

requirements: R1: Asia/Taipei anchoring stays; no hardcoded dates; tolerant parsing preserved
R2: Mock mode intact — mock horizon (7 days anchored today) remains valid under the new validation
R3: No regression of prior cards (pinned chart, exact sunrise, day strip, selected state, hint copy)
R4: English-only code comments; no secrets
acceptance_criteria: AC1: ruff + pytest + frontend tests + npm run build all pass
AC2: Live mode: every rendered day card, including the last one when CWA returns 8 days, is clickable and returns 200 with correct advice/sunrise for that date
AC3: Heading shows 共 N 天 matching the rendered card count
AC4: Simulated click-failure keeps the week view and current selection with an inline error (test-level verification); no reset to today
AC5: Past dates and far-future dates still return 400
AC6: Changes committed per-AC and pushed to origin
operational_notes: Live-mode town codes are CWA-sourced (e.g. cwa-65000110 / cwa-65000260); read from live /api/towns before manual testing.
Live-verified facts at dispatch time: /api/forecast days = [7/5..7/12] (8 entries); requesting date=7/12 returns HTTP 400 under current validation.
Ports 18082/5173 are free at dispatch time; clean up any verification servers afterward.

report_template.location: Ubuntu agent-bridge reports path or repo-local equivalent used by the current workflow

user_one_liner: Align the backend date window with the real rolling CWA horizon so every rendered day card (including an 8th day after evening issuance) is clickable, make the 共 N 天 heading dynamic, and make day-click errors show inline without resetting the page back to today.
next_task_hint: Remaining: deploy auth -> actual public-URL deploy, Gemini decision, submission polish. Deadline 2026-07-06 12:00.
